items.headline: Historic house to be preserved while four townhomes built behind it
items.summary: A development at 434 Crescent Avenue proposes keeping an existing 960-square foot historic home while constructing four three-story townhomes in the rear. The project requires approval to alter the historic resource while maintaining its presence on the lot.

items.headline: City Council approves digital signage agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor
items.summary: The council amended its agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC regarding large format digital signage on city-owned property. This affects what advertisements and messages residents will see on public spaces throughout San José.

items.headline: City seeks proposals for encampment cleanup services
items.summary: The council received a report on requests for proposals for encampment cleanup services, indicating the city is evaluating new approaches or contractors for managing homeless encampments in the area.

items.headline: City negotiating property purchase with school district at Finch Avenue
items.summary: Cupertino is in closed-door negotiations with Cupertino Union School District over purchasing property at 10480 Finch Avenue, with price and payment terms still being worked out. This could signal a potential new city facility, school expansion, or land use change in that area.

items.headline: Milpitas adopts Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
items.summary: The City Council approved a comprehensive master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District area, which will guide future recreation and green space development. This plan shapes what public amenities and outdoor spaces residents will have access to as the district develops.

items.headline: El Paseo De Saratoga: 772-unit housing complex approved
items.summary: Two connected residential buildings totaling 772 units and $125.7M in investment are coming to El Paseo De Saratoga. This is a major housing addition that will significantly expand the area's residential capacity.

items.headline: 91 affordable apartments coming to East Santa Clara
items.summary: New Algarve development will add 91 affordable units at 1135 E Santa Clara St ($10.3M). This addresses San José's critical shortage of below-market housing in a central location.

items.headline: Council votes on nine new homes plus accessory units on Old Trace Road
items.summary: The city is considering a proposal to subdivide a vacant lot into nine single-family homes and seven junior accessory dwelling units (small second units) in the Residential Estate zone. This is a quasi-judicial hearing where the council will decide whether to approve the tentative map for this residential development.
